marketState: neutral, with bearish near-term technicals offset by a contrarian bullish medium-term skew
summary: Front NG is essentially at fair value ($2.859 vs $2.923 fair, well inside the $2.42-3.42 band) so there is no mispricing edge today. Near-term technicals are heavy — 1h/4h/1d/1w all trend bearish with a 50<200 EMA cross on every timeframe and daily/weekly RSI near 35 — while a bearish storage surprise (+22 Bcf vs +19 forecast, +180 Bcf above the 5y average) adds a fresh supply headwind. Medium-term the picture flips more constructive: managed money is net short at just the 8th percentile of its 5-year range (a classic crowded-short/contrarian setup), and 8-16 day weather demand is running +21.7% above the 5-year norm at the 89th percentile of its range, pointing to hotter-than-normal cooling demand into peak season. Seasonality itself is weak and low-reliability (probability of higher prices in 1/3/4 weeks is only 44/38/38%, typical 4-week move -1.4%), so it does not add real conviction either way. Futures-curve data was unavailable in this snapshot, so the contango/backwardation read is a genuine gap.

longTerm.entry: $2.70-2.85 (scale in on dips toward lower fair-value band)
longTerm.sizing: 1R approx 1% account risk; take 50% off at TP1
longTerm.rationale: Managed-money net-short positioning at the 8th 5y percentile is a classic contrarian setup for a squeeze, and 8-16D weather demand at +21.7% vs 5y (89th percentile range position) supports above-normal cooling demand into peak season; price is also trading modestly below the rising fair-value curve at every horizon out to 21 days.
longTerm.invalidation: 8-16D weather forecasts revert to normal/cool, storage surprises stay bearish and the deviation vs 5y keeps widening, or price closes decisively below the $2.45 fair-value band floor.

shortTerm.sizing: 1R approx 0.5-1% account risk (smaller size — daily RSI already near oversold, limiting downside room)
shortTerm.rationale: Four of six technical timeframes (1h/4h/1d/1w) trend bearish with a 50<200 EMA cross across every timeframe, near-term (1-7D) weather demand is only in line with the 5y norm (not supportive), and this week's storage build beat forecast by 22 Bcf (bearish surprise) — fresh short-term negative catalysts with price still sitting at fair value, not cheap.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily/weekly trend flips to neutral/bullish (EMA cross resolves, MACD hist turns positive on 1d/1w), or 1-7D weather demand jumps materially above the 5y norm.

risks: Seasonality reliability is flagged Low (59pp 10-90% spread) — wide historical dispersion, little standalone edge
